data.title: BAE Systems awarded a $1.74B U.S. Navy contract
data.description:
• BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) has been awarded a $1.74B U.S. Navy contract for up to 55,000 Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System II Full Rate Production Lots 13-17. 
  • This contract was not competed. 
  • Naval Air Systems Command, Patuxent River, Maryland, is the contracting activity.

data.title: NMP Acquisition announces separate trading of Class A ordinary shares
data.description:
• NMP Acquisition (NMPAU) said on Thursday that, commencing September 3, 2025, holders of the units sold in the company’s initial public offering may elect to separately trade the Class A ordinary shares and the rights included in those units.
  • The Class A ordinary shares and the rights that are separated are expected to trade on the Global Market tier of Nasdaq under the symbols “NMP” and “NMPAR,” respectively.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ulta Beauty in charts: Net sales climbs on YY basis driven by 6.7% rise in comparable sales
Meta pushes to release new Llama model before 2026: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) plans to release its next artificial intelligence model, which is known internally as Llama 4.X or Llama 4.5, before the end of the year, according to Business Insider.

It is expected to be one of the first projects from Meta Superintelligence Labs, according to the report, which cited people familiar with the issue.

Meta Superintelligence Labs is led by Alexandr Wang, the former CEO of Scale AI, whom Meta hired after making a $14.3B investment for a 49% stake in the company. Meta has reportedly restructured Meta Superintelligence Labs into four groups, with teams focused on training, research, product and infrastructure.

Meta first announced the Llama 4 models in April, which included Maverick, Scout and Behemoth. Whille Scout and Maverick were released on April 5, Behemoth, which was slated for a June release, remains stuck in development.

data.title: Medical Properties Trust announces lease agreement for six California facilities
data.description:
• Medical Properties Trust (NYSE:MPW) announced on Thursday a lease agreement with NOR Healthcare Systems in conjunction with NOR’s successful bid for Prospect Medical Group’s California operations. 
  • The six facilities will be subject to a master lease with initial annualized rent of $45M, virtually identical to Prospect’s once-scheduled 2025 rent, and CPI-based annual rent escalators beginning in 2026. 
  • All rent will be deferred for a period of six months, and 50% of rent will be deferred for an additional period of six months, after which the aggregate deferred rent will be paid over the remaining lease term. 
  • MPT has committed to fund up to $60M in seismic improvements as may be required by California regulators over the next four years, which will increase the lease base and result in additional rent at the prevailing lease yield.

data.title: AAR inks exclusive defense deal with AmSafe Bridport
data.description:
• Aviation services firm AAR (NYSE:AIR) on Thursday said that it has signed a multi-year exclusive defensive distribution agreement with AmSafe Bridport, a TransDigm company.
  • AAR to be exclusive distributor for KC-46 and C-40 platform products in global defense and military aftermarket.
  • Agreement covers direct and indirect sales to U.S. Defense Logistics Agency, U.S. Armed Services, and foreign militaries including Japan.
  • Enhances AAR’s government contract support and customer service via its Government Programs business.
  • Expands AAR’s defense distribution portfolio to include cargo handling products.
  • AIR -0.77% after hours to $75.22
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dell in charts: Revenue from Infrastructure Solutions Group rises 44% to record $16.8B
IREN stock climbs after Q4 adjusted EBITDA tops consensus
data.description:
IREN Limited (NASDAQ:IREN) stock jumped 8.1% in Thursday after-hours trading after the bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner and data center operator posted fiscal Q4 adjusted EBITDA that topped the average analyst estimate.

Meanwhile, the company said it's approaching $1.25B total annualized revenue, with scope for further growth ahead. It estimates more than $1B annualized revenue from bitcoin mining under current mining economics, and $200M-$250M annualized revenue from AI Clouid at 10.9K Nvidia GPUs by December 2025.

"FY25 was a breakout year financially and operationally, with record results that included strong net income and more than 10x EBITDA growth," said Co-Founder and CEO Daniel Roberts.

Q4 adjusted EBITDA of $121.9M, beating the consensus estimate of $111.2M, jumped from $82.9M in Q3 and $2.5M in Q1. Adjusted EBITDA included an unrealized gain on financial instruments of $147.7M in Q4 2025, and Q3 included an unrealized loss on financial instruments of $37.9M.

Q4 revenue of $187.3M, missing the consensus of $187.5M) increased from $144.8M in Q3 and $52.8M in Q1 2025.

Total operating income of -$113.8 vs. -$73.8M in the prior quarter.

Conference  call at 5:00 PM ET.

data.title: U.S. grants FAST-41 coverage to enCore Energy's Dewey-Burdock ISR uranium project
data.description:
enCore Energy (NASDAQ:EU) closed +3.1% on Thursday after the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council announced FAST-41 coverage for the company's Dewey-Burdock in situ recovery uranium project in South Dakota.

enCore Energy (NASDAQ:EU) previously completed significant federal review for the project and applied for FAST-41 coverage to "take advantage of the predictability, transparency and accountability of the program for the remaining permitting milestones," the Permitting Council said.

FAST-41 covered projects are entitled to comprehensive permitting timetables and transparent, collaborative management of those timetables on the Federal Permitting Dashboard.

This project is the ninth mining project to receive FAST-41 coverage since President Trump took office.

data.title: Ulta gives FY25 guidance another glow-up after a strong second quarter
data.description:
Shares of Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) were gaining ground in postmarket trading after the beauty retailer raised its outlook for the full year, reflecting another better-than-expected quarter and progress in its “Ulta Beauty Unleashed” strategy.

“Outstanding top line performance, fueled by growth across all major categories, drove market share growth and better-than-expected profitability,” said Ulta CEO Kecia Steelman.

For the quarter ended August 2, Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) earned a profit of $5.78 per share, up from $5.30 a year ago and $0.74 better than expected. Additionally, the company’s gross profit margin widened 90 basis points to 39.2% versus 38.1% estimates.

Fueled by a 6.7% increase in comparable sales (vs +2.98% estimates), 3.7% gain in transactions, and 2.9% gain in average ticket amount, total sales were up 9.3% to $2.8B compared to $2.61B estimates

For the full year, although Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) continues to brace for near-term economic uncertainties, it raised guidance a second time and now expects sales to be within the range of $12B to $12.1B versus a prior range of $11.5B to $11.7B. The current guidance exceeds the consensus forecast of $11.72B.

Ulta (ULTA) also raised its outlook for comparable sales to up 2.5% to 3.5% from flat to up 1.5%, and operating margin to 11.9% to 12.0% from 11.7% to $11.8% previously. This compares to estimates of +1.92% and 11.9%, respectively.

The company also raised its profit outlook for the year to a range of $23.85 to $24.30 from a prior range of $22.65 to $23.20, the midpoint of which exceeds the consensus estimate of $23.73.

The results fueled a 6% rally in shares late Thursday, setting Ulta (ULTA) up for another 17-month high at Friday’s regular open.
